The sin of Ananias and Sapphira was that they were eager to be seen as important followers, when they were not true followers. Hypocrites may deny themselves, may give up their worldly benefits in one situation, with the hope of gaining something else. They were greedy for the wealth of the world and distrustful of God and his care. They thought they could serve both God and money. They thought to deceive the apostles. The Spirit of God in Peter recognized the principle of unbelief ruling in Ananias’s heart. But whatever Satan might suggest, he could not have filled Ananias's heart with this wickedness if he had not agreed to it. The lie was an attempt to deceive the Spirit of truth, who so clearly spoke and acted through the apostles. Ananias’s crime was not keeping part of the price of the land; he could have kept it all if he wanted to; but his trying to trick the apostles with a terrible lie, from a desire to make a vain show, combined with greed. But if we try to deceive God, we will fatally deceive our own souls. How sad to see those relationships that should encourage one another toward what is good, instead hardening one another in what is evil! And this punishment was truly mercy to many people. It would cause careful self-examination, prayer, and fear of hypocrisy, greed, and vain glory, and it should still do so. It would prevent the growth of false believers. Let us learn from this how hateful falsehood is to the God of truth, and not only avoid a direct lie, but also all advantages from using uncertain words and double meaning in our speech.

The separation of hypocrites through discerning judgments should make those who are sincere draw closer to each other and to the gospel ministry. Whatever tends to the purity and reputation of the church promotes its growth, but only that power which worked such miracles by the apostles can rescue sinners from the power of sin and Satan and add believers to His worshippers. Christ will work through all his faithful servants, and everyone who applies to him will be healed.

There is no prison so dark or strong but God can visit his people in it, and, if he pleases, bring them out. Recoveries from sickness, releases out of trouble, are granted, not that we may enjoy the comforts of life, but that God may be honored with the services of our life. It is not for preachers of Christ’s gospel to retire into corners as long as they can have any opportunity of preaching in the great congregation. They must preach to the lowest, whose souls are as precious to Christ as the souls of the greatest. Speak to all, for all are concerned. Speak as those who resolve to stand by it, to live and die by it. Speak all the words of this heavenly, divine life, in comparison with which the present earthly life does not deserve the name. These words of life, which the Holy Ghost puts into your mouth; the words of the gospel are the words of life; words whereby we may be saved. How wretched are those who are troubled by the success of the gospel! They cannot but see that the word and power of the Lord are against them; and they tremble for the consequences, yet they will go on.

Many people will do something wrong with boldness, yet cannot stand to hear about it later or to have it brought up against them. We cannot expect to be redeemed and healed by Christ unless we give ourselves up to be ruled by him. Faith takes the Savior in all his roles, who came, not to save us in our sins, but to save us from our sins. If Christ had been exalted to give dominion to Israel, the chief priests would have welcomed him. But repentance and forgiveness of sins are blessings they neither valued nor realized they needed; therefore, they did not accept his teachings. Wherever repentance is produced, forgiveness is granted without fail. No one is freed from the guilt and punishment of sin except those who are freed from the power and dominion of sin; who turn away from it and against it. Christ gives repentance by his Spirit working with the word, to awaken the conscience, to work sorrow for sin, and an effective change in the heart and life. The giving of the Holy Ghost is clear evidence that it is God’s will that Christ should be obeyed. And he will surely destroy those who will not have him to reign over them.

The Lord still has all hearts in his hands, and sometimes directs the prudence of the worldly wise so as to restrain persecutors. Common sense tells us to be cautious, while experience and observation show that the success of frauds in matters of religion has been very short. Reproach for Christ is true preferment, as it makes us conformable to his pattern and serviceable to his interest. They rejoiced in it. If we suffer ill for doing well, provided we suffer it well and as we should, we ought to rejoice in that grace which enabled us so to do. The apostles did not preach themselves but Christ. This was the preaching that most offended the priests. But it ought to be the constant business of gospel ministers to preach Christ: Christ, and him crucified; Christ, and him glorified; nothing beside this, but what has reference to it. And whatever is our station or rank in life, we should seek to make Him known and to glorify his name.
